REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. THIS PETITION WAS FILED ON JUNE 17, 2023 SEEKING SPECIFIC RELIEF OUTLINED THEREIN. THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THIS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ENCOMPASSES ISSUES NOT BEFORE THE COURT ON THE PETITION AS CURRENTLY DRAFTED. DUE TO THE PASSAGE OF TIME AND THE EVENTS THAT SUBSEQUENTLY TRANSPIRED (TERMINATION OF SUBSIDY WHICH PETITIONER SEEKS TO REINSTATE BY THE MOTION), THE RELIEF REQUESTED MAY BE FUTILE (2021 RECERTIFICATION) OR MOOT (DISSEMINATION OF PRIVATE INFORMATION IN CONJUNCTION WITH RECERTIFICATION PROCESS). PETITIONER MAY FILE AN AMENDED PETITION AND SEEK APPROPRIATE RELIEF. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS OVERRULED. RESPONDENTS HAVE 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. "THE PRIVATE RIGHT OF CIVIL ACTION CREATED BY THE [PRIVACY] ACT IS SPECIFICALLY LIMITED TO ACTIONS AGAINST AGENCIES OF THE UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT." (UNT V. AEROSPACE CORP. (9TH CIR. 1985) 765 F.2D 1440, 1447.) RESPONDENTS FAIL TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY SHOWING THAT ANY OF THE RESPONDENTS ARE AN "AGENCY," AS DEFINED IN SECTION 552(F)(1). PETITIONER EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES AND THE ISSUES ARE RIPE. WHETHER ANY DUTY REMAINS TO BE DISCHARGED BY RESPONDENTS UNDER THE RELEVANT STATUTES IS NOT DETERMINED ON DEMURRER AND NOT A PERSUASIVE ARGUMENT UNDER SECTION 430.10(F), I.E., "THE PLEADING IS UNCERTAIN." JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. TAUALA ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Kevin Haah; Open Door Legal; 415-938-9203; appearing as counsel for Petitioner Kesha Cromartie.

Not contested. The court rules as follows:

RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. The petition for writ of mandate and all causes of action are sufficiently stated and are not uncertain. Declaration of Ravneet Sandhu is stricken, except for paragraphs 1, 2, 3, and 28.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO AMEND And Notice Of Motion; Declaration Of Kelsey Merrick
Off Calendar Jan-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Kelsey Merrick; Open Door Legal | Bayview; 415-938-9203; appearing as counsel for Petitioner Kesha Cromartie.
Ravneet Sandhu; Law Offices of Vernon C. Goins; 510-663-3700; appearing as counsel for Respondents Northridge Cooperative Homes, Inc., Alton Management Corporation, Kevin Barr, and Tamara Cato.

Respondents contest the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KESHA CROMARTIE'S MOTION TO AMEND AND NOTICE OF MOTION is GRANTED. The Court finds no prejudice to Respondents. Petitioner to file First Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate and Complaint within five (5) court days.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion; Petitioners Motion For Judgment On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Kelsey Merrick; Open Door Legal; 415-938-9203; appearing as counsel for Petitioner Kesh Cromartie.
Ravneet Sandhu; Law Offices of Vernon C. Goins; 510-663-3700; appearing as counsel for Respondents Northridge Cooperative Homes, et al.

Petitioner contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is DENIED. This petition was filed on June 17, 2023 seeking specific relief outlined therein. The relief sought in this motion for judgment encompasses issues not before the Court on the Petition as currently drafted. Due to the passage of time and the events that subsequently transpired (termination of subsidy which Petitioner seeks to reinstate by the motion), the relief requested may be futile (2021 recertification) or moot (dissemination of private information in conjunction with recertification process). Petitioner may file an amended petition and seek appropriate relief.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion; Petitioners Motion For Judgment On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-17-2023 Continued To Aug-11-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PETITIONER KESHA CROMARTIE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is continued to August 11, 2023 on Court's own motion.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Apr-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kelsey Merrick (415)938-9203 of Open Door Legal appearing by Zoom for plaintiff Kesha Cromartie.
Ludis Sominskaia (510)663-3700 of Law Offices of Vernon Goins appearing by Zoom for defendants, Northridge Cooperative Homes, Inc. Et Al.

Hearing held. The Court rules as follows:

RESPONDENT NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES,INC., ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, KEVIN BARR, TAMARA CATO DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
is OVERRULED. Respondents have 10 days to Answer.
"The private right of civil action created by the [Privacy] Act is specifically limited to actions against agencies of the United States Government." (Unt v. Aerospace Corp. (9th Cir. 1985) 765 F.2d 1440, 1447.) Respondents fail to cite any authority showing that any of the Respondents are an "agency," as defined in section 552(f)(1).
Petitioner exhausted her administrative remedies and the issues are ripe.
Whether any duty remains to be discharged by Respondents under the relevant statutes is not determined on demurrer and not a persuasive argument under section 430.10(f), i.e., "The pleading is uncertain."

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)
